children.heading: Determination of misbranding; notification of Federal Trade Commission by Secretary; contents
children.label: a Determination of misbranding; notification of Federal Trade Commission by Secretary; contents
A
children.text: with respect to any food which the Secretary determines is misbranded under because of its advertising, orsection 343(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a food’s advertising which the Secretary determines causes the food to be so misbranded,

children.label: A
children.text: contain (i) a description of the action the Secretary proposes to take and of the advertising which the Secretary has determined causes a food to be misbranded, (ii) a statement of the reasons for the Secretary’s determination that such advertising has caused such food to be misbranded, and

children.label: B
children.text: be accompanied by the records, documents, and other written materials which the Secretary determines supports his determination that such food is misbranded because of such advertising.

children.heading: Action by Federal Trade Commission precluding action by Secretary; exception
children.label: b Action by Federal Trade Commission precluding action by Secretary; exception
A
children.text: it has initiated under the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] an investigation of such advertising to determine if it is prohibited by such Act or any order or rule under such Act,15 U.S.C. 41

children.label: B
children.text: it has commenced (or intends to commence) a civil action under section 5, 13, or 19 [, 53, or 57b] with respect to such advertising or the Attorney General has commenced (or intends to commence) a civil action under section 5 [] with respect to such advertising,15 U.S.C. 4515 U.S.C. 45

children.label: C
children.text: it has issued and served (or intends to issue and serve) a complaint under section 5(b) of such Act [] respecting such advertising, or15 U.S.C. 45(b)

children.label: D
children.text: pursuant to section 16(b) of such Act [] it has made a certification to the Attorney General respecting such advertising,15 U.S.C. 56(b)

children.label: A
children.text: the Commission or the Attorney General does not commence a civil action described in subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) of this subsection respecting the advertising described in the Secretary’s notice,

children.label: B
children.text: the Commission does not issue and serve a complaint described in subparagraph (C) of such paragraph respecting such advertising, or

children.label: C
children.text: the Commission does not (as described in subparagraph (D) of such paragraph) make a certification to the Attorney General respecting such advertising, or, if the Commission does make such a certification to the Attorney General respecting such advertising, the Attorney General, before the expiration of such period, does not cause appropriate criminal proceedings to be brought against such advertising,

children.heading: Secretary’s determination of imminent hazard to health as suspending applicability of provisions
children.label: c Secretary’s determination of imminent hazard to health as suspending applicability of provisions
children.text: The requirements of subsections (a) and (b) do not apply with respect to action under subchapter III with respect to any food or food advertising if the Secretary determines that such action is required to eliminate an imminent hazard to health.

children.heading: Coordination of action by Secretary with Federal Trade Commission
children.label: d Coordination of action by Secretary with Federal Trade Commission
children.text: For the purpose of avoiding unnecessary duplication, the Secretary shall coordinate any action taken under subchapter III because of advertising which the Secretary determines causes a food to be misbranded with any action of the Federal Trade Commission under the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] with respect to such advertising.15 U.S.C. 41
